thread_title: Video Self-Modeling as a Behavioral Intervention
seeneyu_relevance: seeneyu users record themselves, then receive AI-annotated feedback on their own video. This is a clinical Video Self-Modeling (VSM) intervention. Nearly 200 published studies confirm VSM produces measurable behavioral change — including communication skills, motor skill acquisition, and self-efficacy.
hypothesis: Watching recordings of oneself performing a target behavior accelerates skill acquisition faster than instruction alone, because it leverages self-efficacy, observational learning, and direct behavioral feedback simultaneously.
key_finding: VSM is an evidence-based intervention (nearly 200 studies over 3 decades) showing efficacy across communication, motor, social, and academic domains. Improvements generalize across settings and are maintained over time.
evidence_grade_summary: B — Supported by multiple controlled studies, school-based systematic reviews, and meta-analyses, particularly in communication skills and motor learning.

sources.title: A review of self-modeling and related interventions
sources.key_finding: Comprehensive review of ~150 self-modeling studies. VSM produces behavioral change faster than instruction alone. Two mechanisms: feedforward (watching future-capable self) and self-review (watching best current performance). Both show efficacy.
sources.seeneyu_application: seeneyu's AI feedback loop is a form of self-review VSM: users watch their recordings with annotated overlays highlighting best-performing moments and areas to improve.
sources.quote: Self-modeling interventions using only positive imagery fit well into positive behavior support plans and consistently produce faster skill acquisition than instruction-only approaches.

sources.title: The use of self-modeling to improve the swimming performance of spina bifida children
sources.key_finding: Original VSM study: edited video replay (showing only positive behaviors) improved motor skills in children with physical disabilities. Behavioral improvements observed in close association with intervention with continuing gains on reapplication.
sources.seeneyu_application: Establishes VSM as effective for motor skill acquisition — the same class of skills as body language/posture. Foundation paper of the VSM literature.

sources.title: Video self-modeling intervention in school-based settings: A review
sources.key_finding: Systematic review confirming VSM functional control of targeted academic and communication behaviors. Evidence for generalization across settings and maintenance over time. VSM works for communication skill acquisition.
sources.seeneyu_application: Communication skill generalization — skills learned through VSM transfer to real-world contexts, not just the practice setting. This directly supports seeneyu's core promise.

sources.title: Video feedforward for reading
sources.key_finding: Reading fluency improved significantly for all students. Rate of improvement was significantly greatest during feedforward VSM in 9 out of 10 cases. Watching yourself succeed at something you're trying to learn accelerates acquisition.
sources.seeneyu_application: The feedforward mechanism directly maps to seeneyu: users watch themselves performing the target behavior (body language) in the context of a reference clip, then practice. The model of 'watching yourself succeed' builds self-efficacy before practice.
sources.quote: Rate of improvement was significantly greatest during feedforward in 9 out of 10 cases.

sources.title: A meta-analysis of video modeling and video self-modeling interventions for children and adolescents with autism spectrum disorders
sources.key_finding: Meta-analysis: VSM and video modeling are effective and efficient interventions. Effect maintained at follow-up. Social-communication skills specifically showed strong effect sizes. Intervention effects generalize to naturalistic settings.
sources.seeneyu_application: Social-communication skills — the exact domain seeneyu targets — show the strongest VSM effect sizes. Directly validates the product category.

sources.title: Effects of video self-modeling on spontaneous requesting in children with autism
sources.key_finding: Increases in confidence, self-efficacy, and motivation are secondary outcomes of VSM beyond the primary target skill. Users become more motivated to practice when they see themselves succeeding.
sources.seeneyu_application: Motivation and self-efficacy gains from VSM directly support user retention. Seeing your own AI-scored progress builds intrinsic motivation to continue — supporting seeneyu's gamification layer.
